concepts.title: Turán's theorem and the Turán graph
concepts.blurb: $\mathrm{ex}(n, K_{r+1}) = (1 - 1/r)\,n^2/2 - O(1)$, with the unique extremal graph the balanced complete $r$-partite Turán graph $T(n, r)$. Proof by Zykov symmetrization; the limiting Turán density $\pi(K_{r+1}) = 1 - 1/r$.

concepts.title: Kővári–Sós–Turán & the Zarankiewicz problem
concepts.blurb: $\mathrm{ex}(n, K_{s,t}) = O(n^{2-1/s})$ via a double-counting (cherries / Jensen) argument. The bipartite Zarankiewicz problem asks for $0/1$-matrices avoiding all-ones $s\times t$ blocks; the Erdős–Rényi polarity construction matches KST up to constants for $s=t=2$.

concepts.title: Erdős–Stone–Simonovits: the chromatic threshold
concepts.blurb: $\mathrm{ex}(n, H) = (1 - 1/(\chi(H)-1))\,n^2/2 + o(n^2)$. The Turán density of any non-bipartite $H$ depends only on its chromatic number; bipartite $H$ has density $0$, putting all the action into KST-style sub-quadratic exponents.

concepts.title: Ramsey numbers
concepts.blurb: $R(k,\ell)$: smallest $n$ such that every $2$-edge-coloring of $K_n$ contains a monochromatic $K_k$ or $K_\ell$. Pigeonhole gives $R(3,3)=6$; Erdős's probabilistic lower bound gives $R(k,k)\ge \sqrt{2}^k$, $4^k$ above. Schur's theorem on sum-free partitions is an arithmetic Ramsey corollary.

concepts.title: Sperner's theorem and the LYM inequality
concepts.blurb: An antichain in the Boolean lattice $2^{[n]}$ has size at most $\binom{n}{\lfloor n/2\rfloor}$, attained by the middle layer. Proof via the LYM inequality $\sum_{F\in\mathcal{F}} 1/\binom{n}{|F|}\le 1$, by counting maximal chains.

concepts.title: Triangle removal lemma and Szemerédi regularity
concepts.blurb: Ruzsa–Szemerédi: a graph with $o(n^3)$ triangles can be made triangle-free by deleting $o(n^2)$ edges; equivalently, robust graphs are supersaturated. Szemerédi's regularity lemma decomposes any dense graph into $\varepsilon$-regular pairs, with bounds tower-type in $\varepsilon^{-1}$. Together these give Roth's theorem on $3$-APs.
